grant_title: Maine Senior Assistance Programs, Benefits, and Grants (2026)
eligible_applicant_type: Low-income older adults (earning less than 200% of federal poverty level)
Seniors requiring nursing home level of care (for HCBS Waiver)
Seniors facing winter heating crises (for HEAP)
geographic_restrictions: Maine residents only; rural and urban areas both eligible, with some programs prioritizing rural applicants due to geographic isolation.
project_area: Senior assistance programs covering property tax relief, healthcare, housing, food, winter heating, transportation, legal aid, and veteran benefits.
demographic_requirements: Age 60+ for most programs; some programs require age 65+ (e.g., Property Tax Fairness Credit, HCBS Waiver). Income limits vary by program (e.g., below $2,901/month for HCBS Waiver, below $1,677/month for MaineCare). Disability status may qualify applicants for specific programs (e.g., HCBS Waiver). Veterans must have honorable discharge for veteran-specific programs.
eligibility_summary: Eligibility varies by program but generally requires Maine residency, age 60+ (or 65+ for some programs), income below specified limits, and sometimes asset limits. Priority is given to low-income seniors, disabled individuals, veterans, and those facing winter heating crises. Some programs have waiting lists (e.g., HCBS Waiver) or seasonal deadlines (e.g., HEAP).
